spec.description: The percentage of the time during which Scylla utilized the CPU. Note that because Scylla does busy polling for some time before going idle, CPU utilization as seen by the operating system may be much higher. Your system is not yet CPU-bottlenecked until this metric is high.
spec.title: Load

spec.description: Background writes are writes that are already acknowledged to the application but have additional work to be done. For instance, if a replica responded and only one is needed, this request is still listed as a background request until all replicas respond. This metric represents a queue size, not a rate. High values here correlate with increased write latencies.
spec.title: Background Writes $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Amount of cache row tombstones read
spec.title: Cache Row Tombstones reads $func by [[by]]

spec.description: The number of tombstones found in SSTables. This number is updated by an SSTable update after flush, compaction, or streaming operations.
spec.title: Tombstones found in SSTables $func by [[by]]

spec.description: The number of range tombstones found in SSTables. This number is updated by an SSTable update after flush, compaction, or streaming operations.
spec.title: Range Tombstones found in SSTables $func by [[by]]

spec.description: The number of Cell Tombstones found in SSTables. This number is updated by an SSTable update after flush, compaction, or streaming operations.
spec.title: Cell Tombstones found in SSTables $func by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_latency_summary_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))  by ([[by]]) or on([[by]]) $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))  by ([[by]]))

$topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(casrlatencya{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^", by="[[by]]", scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}) by([[by]]) or on([[by]]) $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_latency_sum{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])/($func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) + 1))

$topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_write_latency_summary_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on ([[by]]) ($func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_write_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])))

spec.description: Foreground reads are reads that weren't acknowledged yet to the application. For instance, if a single replica responded and two are needed due to the consistency level. This metric represents a queue size, not a rate. High values here correlate with increased read latencies.
spec.title: Foreground Reads $func by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(caswlatencya{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^", by="[[by]]", scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}) by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name) or on([[by]], scheduling_group_name) ($func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_write_latency_sum{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name)/($func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_write_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name) + 1)))

spec.description: A single Read/Write LWT will result in multiple paxos operations
spec.title: Paxos operations $func by [[by]]

spec.description: How many paxos operations that did not yet produce a result are running
spec.title: Paxos Foreground operations $func by [[by]]

spec.description: How many paxos operations are still running after a result was already returned
spec.title: Paxos Background operations $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
An LWT INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE command that involves a condition will be rejected if the condition is not met.

While it is ok, a high value may indicate that there is a potential problem with data distribution
spec.title: LWT Condition-Not-Met $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of times some INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE request with conditions had to retry because there was a concurrent conditional statement against the same key. Each retry is performed after a randomized sleep interval, so it can lead to statement timing out completely.

It can indicates contention over a hot row or key
spec.title: LWT Write Contention $func by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_contention_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) - $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_contention_bucket{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^", le="1.000000"}[$__rate_interval]))  by ([[by]]))

spec.description:
Number of times some SELECT with SERIAL consistency had to retry because there was a concurrent conditional statement against the same key. Each retry is performed after a randomized sleep interval, so it can lead to statement timing out completely.

It can indicates contention over a hot row or key
spec.title: LWT Read Contention $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Background reads are reads that are already acknowledged to the application but have additional work to be done. For instance, if a replica responded and only one is needed, this request is still listed as a background request until all replicas respond. This metric represents a queue size, not a rate. High values here correlate with increased read latencies.
spec.title: Background Reads $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Number of partially succeeded conditional statements. These statements were not committed by the coordinator, due to some replicas responding with errors or timing out. The coordinator had to propagate the error to the client. However, the statement succeeded on a minority of replicas, so may later be propagated to the rest during repair.
spec.title: LWT Write Timeout Due to Uncertainty $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Number of times a INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE with conditions failed after being unable to contact enough replicas to match the consistency level
spec.title: LWT Write Unavailable $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Number of times a SELECT with SERIAL consistency failed after being unable to contact enough replicas to match the consistency level
spec.title: LWT Read Unavailable $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of Paxos-repairs of INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE with conditions.

A repair is necessary when a previous Paxos statement was partially successful. A subsequent statement then may not proceed before completing the work of its predecessor. A repair is not guaranteed to succeed, the metric indicates the number of repair attempts made
spec.title: LWT Write Unfinished - Repair Attempts $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of Paxos-repairs of SELECT statement with SERIAL consistency.

A repair is necessary when a previous Paxos statement was partially successful. A subsequent statement then may not proceed before completing the work of its predecessor. A repair is not guaranteed to succeed, the metric indicates the number of repair attempts made
spec.title: LWT Read Unfinished - Repair Attempts $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Normally, a PREPARE Paxos-round piggy-backs the previous value along with the PREPARE response. When the coordinator is unable to obtain the previous value (or its digest) from some of the participants, or when the digests did not match, a separate repair round has to be performed.

This indicates that some Paxos queries did not run successfully to completion, e.g. because some node is overloaded, down, or there was contention around a key.
spec.title: Failed Read-Round Optimization $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of pruning requests.

A successful conditional statement deletes the intermediate state from system.paxos table using PRUNE command.
spec.title: LWT Prune $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of Dropped pruning requests.

A successful conditional statement deletes the intermediate state from system.paxos table using PRUNE command. If the system is busy it may not keep up with the PRUNE requests, so such requests are dropped.

High value suggests the system is overloaded and also that system.paxos table is taking up space. If a prune is dropped, system.paxos table key and value for respective LWT transaction  will stay around until next transaction against the same key or until the gc_grace_period, when it's removed by compaction.
spec.title: LWT Dropped Prune $func by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_cdc_operations_failed{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])/($func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_latency_summary_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on ([[by]]) $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) + 1))

spec.description: The rate of failed CDC operations.
spec.title: Failed CDC operations $func by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(scylla_sstables_bloom_filter_memory_size{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}) by ([[by]])/$func(scylla_memory_total_memory{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}) by ([[by]]))

spec.description:
Bloom filter should take a small percentage of the total memory.

scylla_sstables_bloom_filter_memory_size
spec.title: Bloom Filter memory usage (percentage )

spec.description: Percentage of CPU time used by compaction
spec.title: Compactions CPU Runtime

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_write_latency_summary_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on([[by]]) $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_write_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]))

$func(wlatencyp99{by="instance,shard", instance=~"[[node]]|$^",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"})by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name)/(scalar(count(count(scylla_reactor_utilization) by ([[by]])) != bool count(scylla_reactor_utilization))* scalar($func(count(scylla_reactor_utilization) by (instance,shard)) !=bool  count(scylla_reactor_utilization)))

$func(rlatencyp95{by="instance,shard", instance=~"[[node]]|$^",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"})by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name)/(scalar(count(count(scylla_reactor_utilization) by ([[by]])) != bool count(scylla_reactor_utilization))* scalar($func(count(scylla_reactor_utilization) by (instance,shard)) !=bool  count(scylla_reactor_utilization)))

$func(rlatencyp99{by="instance,shard", instance=~"[[node]]|$^",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"})by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name)/(scalar(count(count(scylla_reactor_utilization) by ([[by]])) != bool count(scylla_reactor_utilization))* scalar($func(count(scylla_reactor_utilization) by (instance,shard)) !=bool  count(scylla_reactor_utilization)))

spec.description:
Number of unexpected errors during sending, sending will be retried later

scylla_hints_manager_send_errors
spec.title: Hints-Send Errors $func by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(rate(scylla_transport_cql_request_bytes{kind=~"QUERY|EXECUTE",instance=~"[[node]]|^$",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])/(sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_write_latency_summary_count{instance=~"[[node]]|^$",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on([[by]]) sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_write_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]|^$",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])))

spec.description:
This is a ballpark estimation of the write-messages size (like insert and update).

It is based on the assumption that write-messages are responsible for most inwards traffic.
spec.title: Estimated write message size by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(rate(scylla_transport_cql_response_bytes{kind=~"QUERY|EXECUTE",instance=~"[[node]]|^$",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])/(sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_read_latency_summary_count{instance=~"[[node]]|^$",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on ([[by]]) sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_read_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]|^$",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^",scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])))

spec.description:
This is a ballpark estimation of the read-messages size (like select).

It is based on the assumption that read-messages are responsible for most outbound traffic.
spec.title: Estimated read message size by [[by]]

options.content: Scylla Monitoring version - 4.16.0-rc0

spec.description:
Number of times a digest read was done on behalf of a speculative retry.

Speculative retry is a mechanism that causes the client or server to speculate that a request may fail, and send a new request.

speculative retry may reduce latency in exchange for system load, but only if there is little activity.

A lot of speculative retries increases load and can harm latency more than helping.
spec.title: Speculative Digest Reads $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of times a read was done on behalf of a speculative retry.

Speculative retry is a mechanism that causes the client or server to speculate that a request may fail, and send a new request.

speculative retry may reduce latency in exchange for system load, but only if there is little activity.

A lot of speculative retries increases load and can harm latency more than helping.
spec.title: Speculative Data Reads $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Amount of requests served as the coordinator. Imbalances here represent dispersion at the connection level, not your data model.
spec.title: Requests Served $func by [[by]] - Coordinator

options.content: Timeouts and Errors - Coordinator

spec.description: Requests that Scylla tried to write but timed out. Timeouts are counted in the node that received the request (the coordinator), not at the replicas.
spec.title: Write Timeouts/Seconds $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Requests that Scylla did not even try to write because replicas that were needed to execute this write were unavailable. Unavailable writes are counted in the node that received the request (the coordinator), not at the replicas.
spec.title: Write Unavailable/Seconds $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Requests that Scylla tried to read but timed out. Timeouts are counted in the node that received the request (the coordinator), not at the replicas.
spec.title: Read Timeouts/Seconds $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Requests that Scylla did not even try to read because replicas that were needed to execute this write were unavailable. Unavailable reads are counted in the node that received the request (the coordinator), not at the replicas.
spec.title: Read Unavailable/Seconds $func by [[by]]

spec.description: The number of currently active read operations
spec.title: Active reads $func by [[by]]

spec.description: number of currently queued read operations
spec.title: Queued reads $func by [[by]]

spec.description: The current number of requests blocked due to reaching the memory quota. Non-zero value indicates that our bottleneck is memory
spec.title: Writes currently blocked on dirty $func by [[by]]

spec.description: number of currently pending allocations. A non-zero value indicates that we have a bottleneck in the disk write flow.
spec.title: Writes currently blocked on commitlog $func by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], clamp_max(1 + sum((rate(scylla_cache_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]) - rate(scylla_cache_reads_with_misses{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) by ([[by]])/(sum(rate(scylla_cache_reads_with_misses{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) + 0.00001),100))

spec.description:
Reciprocal Miss Rate is a score in the range of 1 to 100 that is used to decide the fraction of read requests to send to each replica - a replica with twice the RMR value of another replica will serve twice the number of read requests.

RMR is calculated on a table level, this is an aggregate estimation of that score.
spec.title: Reciprocal Miss Rate (HWLB)

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(rate(scylla_rpc_compression_compressed_bytes_sent{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", algorithm!~"raw"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]], algorithm)/sum(rate(scylla_rpc_compression_bytes_sent{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", algorithm!~"raw"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]], algorithm))

spec.description:
Data compression percentage by using compressed algorithm

scylla_rpc_compression_compressed_bytes_sent
spec.title: Compression Ratios by Algorithm
Compressed Bytes Sent by Algorithm $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of sstables currently open for reading 

scylla_sstables_currently_open_for_reading
spec.title: Open sstables for reading $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of sstables disk reads

scylla_database_disk_reads
spec.title: SStables disk reads $func by [[by]]

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_s3_total_write_latency_sec{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster=~"$cluster|$^", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])/$func(rate(scylla_s3_total_write_requests{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster=~"$cluster|$^", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]))

$topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_s3_total_read_latency_sec{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster=~"$cluster|$^", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])/$func(rate(scylla_s3_total_read_requests{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster=~"$cluster|$^", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]))

spec.description: number of reads that were served from the cache
spec.title: Reads with no misses $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Represents the ‘load’ value the balancer equalizes across instances.

It is based on the number of tablets assigned to each instance, adjusted so larger disks are expected to hold proportionally more tablets.

scylla_load_balancer_load
spec.title: Tablets load by [[by]]
Authorized Prepared Statements Cache Eviction $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
The number of entries currently in the querier cache.

spec.description:
Counts querier cache lookups (paging queries)

spec.description:
Counts querier cache lookups that failed to find a cached querier

scylla_database_querier_cache_misses
spec.title: Querier Cache Misses $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Counts sstables checked after applying the bloom filter. High value indicates that bloom filter is not very efficient.
spec.title: SStables checked after applying the bloom filter $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Counts sstables that survived the clustering key filtering. High value indicates that bloom filter is not very efficient and still have to access a lot of sstables to get data.
spec.title: SStables that survived the clustering key filtering $func by [[by]]
sstable index file page cache used bytes $func by [[by]]
sstable index file page cache hits $func by [[by]]

sstable partition index cache populations $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Counts the number of view updates generated locally and applied on the same node
spec.title: Local view updates $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Foreground writes are writes that weren't acknowledged yet to the application. For instance, if a single replica responded and two are needed due to the consistency level. This metric represents a queue size, not a rate. High values here correlate with increased write latencies.
spec.title: Foreground Writes $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Counts the number of view updates generated and then sent to be applied on a remote replica
spec.title: Remote view updates $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Size in bytes of the view update backlog at each base replica.
spec.title: View Update Backlog $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Number of dropped view updates due to an excessive view update backlog.
spec.title: Dropped View Updates $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of successfully written hints.

scylla_hints_for_views_manager_written
spec.title: Hints written for view $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Number of hints sent for view.
spec.title: Hints for view $func by [[by]]

spec.description:
Number of unexpected errors during sending, sending will be retried later.

scylla_hints_for_views_manager_send_errors
spec.title: Hints-Send Errors for view $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Currently throttled base writes, as a consequence of the respective view update backlog.
spec.title: Throttled Base Writes $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Amount of range tombstones processed during read.
spec.title: Range Tombstones reads $func by [[by]]

spec.description: Amount of range tombstones processed during read.
spec.title: Cache Range Tombstones Read $func by [[by]]
